sections.paragraph: an unused root, i.q. צָחָה, צָחַח (which see), pr. to be white, shining; hence to be sunny, shone upon and dried up by the sun (Syr. and Ch. צְהָא to be thirsty). Hence צִי, צִיָּה, צִיִּי, צִיֹּון [under צָיָה in Thes.]. In Arabic, the middle radical ה being softened, there is صوى, صوا to dry up; and in Hebrew צָיָה, in the noun צָיֹון.
